provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content: This Act may be cited as the National Emblems Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In this Act unless the context otherwise requires— " Coat of Arms " means the Coat of Arms of the United Republic of Tanzania of the likeness specified in the Second Schedule to this Act and includes any emblem, seal, insignia or ensign of the United Republic; " likeness " includes a photographic or pictorial reproduction, a sketch, a painting or any other form of reproduction or representation and of any size or shape; " Minister " means the Minister for the time being responsible for Home Affairs; " National Flag " means a flag of the description specified in the First Schedule to this Act; " public seal " means a seal of the description specified in the Third Schedule to this Act. Part II – The National Flag and the Coat of Arms

provisions.title: National Flag
provisions.content: With effect from the 26th April 1964, the National Flag of the United Republic of Tanzania shall be of the description specified in the first Schedule.

provisions.title: Coat of Arms
provisions.content: With effect from the 26th April 1964, the Coat of Arms of the United Republic of Tanzania shall be of the description specified in the Second Schedule.

provisions.title: Power of President to modify National Flag or Coat of Arms
provisions.content: The President may, by Proclamation, amend the First or the Second Schedule to this Act to modify the National Flag or, as the case may be, the Coat of Arms .

provisions.title: Unlawful use of National Flag , Coat of Arms or any likeness thereof
provisions.content: (1) No person shall use the National Flag , the Coat of Arms or any likeness of the National Flag or of the Coat of Arms — (a) as a trade mark for any article sold or offered for sale; (b) in furtherance of or as an advertisement for any trade, business, industry, calling or profession; (c) on any article which is sold, offered for sale or intended to be sold or offered for sale; (d) on any article which is used by any person otherwise than for a purpose approved by the Minister . (2) The Minister may, by writing under his hand, exempt any person or class of persons or any article or category of articles from any or all of the provisions of subsection (1), subject to such terms and conditions as the Minister may think fit to impose. (3) Any person who contravenes the provisions of subsection (1) or, being a person to whom the provisions of subsection (2) apply, contravenes any of the terms or conditions imposed by the Minister commits an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding twenty thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years or both.

provisions.title: Insulting the National Flag or Coat of Arms
provisions.content: Any person who does any act or utters any word or publishes any writing with intent to insult or bring into contempt or ridicule the National Flag or Coat of Arms of the United Republic of Tanzania or of any likeness thereof commits an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding twenty thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years or both.

provisions.title: Consent of D.P.P. to prosecute
provisions.content: No person shall be prosecuted for an offence under this Act without the consent in writing of the Director of Public Prosecutions.

provisions.title: Amendment of Cap. 16
provisions.content: [Repeals section 124A of the Penal Code.] Part III – The public seal

provisions.title: Proclamation of public seal
provisions.content: (1) The public seal shall be in of the description specified in the Third Schedule to this Act. (2) The President may, by Proclamation, prescribe any other form of the public seal .

provisions.title: Custody of the public seal
provisions.content: (1) The President may appoint a person holding office in the service of the United Republic to be the Custodian of the public seal and may give directions as to the manner in which it shall be kept and used. (2) In default of any appointment under subsection (1), the President shall be the Custodian of the Public Seal.

provisions.title: Authentication
provisions.content: Whenever the public seal is affixed to any document or other thing, the affixing of the seal shall be authenticated by the signature of the custodian of the seal. First Schedule (Section 12) Design of National Flag A rectangular flag divided into two equal portions diagonally from the right-hand upper corner to the left-hand lower corner; the upper portion of green colour and the lower portion of blue colour; the dividing bands being of golden, black and golden collars; the two golden bands of equal size and smaller than the central black band; of the following design: Second Schedule Design of Coat of Arms Third Schedule Design of public seal History of this document 31 December 2023 Chapter 10 Revised Laws 2023 Consolidation 31 July 2002 this version Chapter 10 Revised Laws 2002 Consolidation 07 May 1971 Commenced 09 December 1962 Commenced Related documents More documents like this one Subsidiary legislation Title National Emblems (Exemption of all Business Entities) Instrument, 2006 Repealed Government Notice 156 of 2006 National Flag and Coat of Arms (Exemption) Instrument, 2005 Government Notice 290 of 2005 National Flag and Coat of Arms (Exemption) Instrument, 2004 Government Notice 174 of 2004 National Flag and Coat of Arms (Exemption) Instrument 2003 Government Notice 162 of 2003 National Flag and Coat of Arms (Exemption) Instrument 2002 Government Notice 342 of 2002 National Flag and Coat of Arms (Exemption) Instrument 2002 Government Notice 341 of 2002 National Flag and Coat of Arms (Exemption) Instrument 2002 Government Notice 340 of 2002 National Flag and Coat of Arm (Exemption) Instrument, 2002 Government Notice 185 of 2002 National Flag and Coat of Arms (Exemption) Instrument, 2002 Government Notice 95 of 2002 [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
